It doesn't change anything, any commitments whatsoever on their end.
Unless it was just the first 30 days, and that's it.
Like, just the first month is the only time they could opt-out.
The rest, they're stuck after that 30 days.
You've been working with us for 14 months.
There's no opt-out period.
Like, they know how we work.
We've done so much for them.
They've been a loss leader for them.
If they want to churn, they need give us notice right the  now if they're going to churn.
And that's really bad, and that shouldn't happen.
But we can do any work for them, and we can build custom workflows.
We're doing work for Lovable.
We're doing a template library for Lovable.
We can build calculators for them.
We can, like, refresh every  page on their site.
We can, like, whatever, right?
Like, they are a billion-a-year ARR company.
I know the CEO, and I know the CTO is an investor.
There's no scenario here.
Where they need a  30 days out at all.
Eric tried to hire me as a CMO of the company a while back.
There's no scenario here where it's like they were paying, gee, $2,500 an hour for his consulting and advisory.
That is four hours a month is what they're paying us essentially, right?
So it's like they hire me to go train their entire company to do AI enablement and training.
I've five sessions for their entire company on like how to build AI workflows and things like that, you know?
So it's like it's just we let the relationship like get to a point where they felt like it was just like, nah.
Like they're not seeing the value and we lost touch with the Riot stakeholder, you know?

Okay, Mara, we've got to find a way to get Victoria on board with like what we need and make it clear that if we can't, you know, find a path forward, a conversation between Marcel and George has to happen.
Sooner rather than later.
She's already committed to annual.
It's like the only negotiation point here is no 30-day opt-out.
There's no opt-out.
This is the annual.
That's it.
And I think if we...
Okay.
And getting the ramp.com work back in and ideally getting it from 9 to 12, right?
Like, we got to do that too.
I don't care.
Just like sign the annual flat.
I don't care.
She has a budget for it.
Go to that.
Say, we've been working with for a while.
Like, there's no question on what we can deliver and what we can do.
We've done so much for you.
Like, and we should freaking summarize that and quantify what we've done for them.
Like, we have to do that as part of renewal process.
Like, if they're not understanding that, and George didn't even know what we were doing last time I talked to him, like, oh, I don't really know.
I'm really not that connected to the work.
Like, you know?
And then Luke got Ken.
And it's like, you know.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
And flexibility to do cashflowdesk or ramp.com.
Like.
That's why, that's even more reason why you don't need an opt-out.
If you're not liking what's happening on Cashflow Desk, which you will, we can just continue to do the great work that we're doing today.
Okay, okay.

I think too, if we can just make it as like non, like as soft as possible in the sense of like, hey, we can't do the 30-day opt-out, but like really excited to keep working with you, capitalizing on all the work, like reminding them of the work that we've done.
I would like make it as non much of an issue as possible because I don't think we'll get a ton of resistance from them unless we present it as a big deal.
Like I don't, so pushback, but I think if we just like say, hey, we can't do this part, but yes, we can meet your other demands.
I think they'll be excited about it.
But if we phrase it, like, you know what I mean?
Yeah, I'll draft a message today, Mara.
And if you think it's better to come from you versus me, I can have you send the message, but I'll draft it.
And then like, let's.
Just get Victoria to thumbs up something that is in lines with what we're talking about here.
And from there, we can get SoW working through and the SoW will be better than the current SoW that we have in terms of the terms.
Yeah, sounds great.
And yeah, I think they mentioned someone new is joining this week or next week.
So if we can get it wrapped up by like end of week, I think that would probably be a good goal.
Yes.
Okay.
Thanks for the direction.
Yeah.
Mara, that's your P0, nothing more important.
Yeah, totally.
hear you.
All right.
Thanks, guys.
Thank you.
Thanks, everyone.-bye.-bye.-bye.
Bye Bye Bye

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Define the renewal strategy for the critical Ramp account.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Imperative:** Retain Ramp's `.com` work at all costs. Losing it to competitor AirOps is an unacceptable investor narrative risk, not a financial one.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=576.0)
  - [**Renewal Offer:** Propose a flat $9k/mo annual contract. This price is non-negotiable as it matches Ramp's stated budget, but the term must be annual with no opt-out.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1747.0)
  - [**Scope & Urgency:** The scope must be flexible to include both the new Cash Flow Desk project and the existing `.com` work. The deal must be closed by EOW before a new stakeholder joins.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=2296.0)
  - [**Escalation Plan:** If Victoria resists the annual term, Marcel will personally escalate to George, Ramp's budget holder, to secure the renewal.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1204.0)

## Topics

### Ramp Account Status & Risk

  - [**Project Shift:** Ramp paused the high-performing vendor pages project, shifting focus to a new "Cash Flow Desk" initiative.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=322.0)
  - [**Competitive Threat:** The internal team (Victoria, Matt) is siloed and lacks strategic visibility. The shift is suspected to be a move to competitor AirOps, which Ramp has used before.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
  - [**Leadership Vacuum:** The recent departure of Luke (Victoria's manager) has created a leadership vacuum, leaving the team without a strategic champion. A new hire is expected soon.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [**Renewal Deadline:** The current contract expires Nov 14. Its 3-month cycles with a "cancel anytime" clause are a major risk.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)

### Renewal Negotiation & Counter-Offer

  - [**Initial Proposal:**](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=822.0)
      - [Cash Flow Desk only: $18k setup + $9k/mo](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)
      - [Both projects: $18k/mo](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)
  - [**Ramp's Counter-Offer:**](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
      - [Cash Flow Desk only: $18k setup + $9k/mo](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)
      - [Scope: 10 articles/week (up from our 5/week quote)](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
      - [Term: Annual contract with a 30-day opt-out clause](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
  - [**Analysis:** Ramp's counter-offer confirms a $9k/mo budget and a desire for an opt-out due to the project's experimental nature.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)

### Strategic Directive & Final Offer

  - [**Primary Goal:** Retain the Ramp logo and `.com` work. The financial terms are secondary to the strategic value.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1390.0)
  - [**Final Offer:**](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1747.0)
      - [**Price:** Flat $9k/mo. This matches Ramp's stated budget and avoids a difficult price increase negotiation.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1747.0)
      - [**Term:** Annual contract with **no opt-out clause**. This is non-negotiable, as an opt-out defeats the purpose of an annual commitment.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1670.0)
      - [**Scope:** Flexible to include both the Cash Flow Desk and `.com` work. This provides Ramp with a low-risk option to continue high-value work if the new project falters.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=2215.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Ramp is a 14-month client with a proven track record. The risk is minimal, and an opt-out is unnecessary.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1703.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Draft the renewal message for Victoria today, outlining the final offer: $9k/mo annual contract with flexible scope and no opt-out.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=2270.0)
  - [**Mara:** Send the message to Victoria by EOW, framing the offer positively and emphasizing the long-term partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=2296.0)
  - [**Andi:** Prepare to escalate to George if Victoria resists the annual term.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1204.0)
  - [**Marcel:** Personally engage with George if escalation is needed.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=1204.0)
  - [**Team:** Quantify and summarize all past work for Ramp to reinforce the value delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/ZXsrByHp_D3pbxsaxNzb7ur-G8ATqgrz?tab=summary&timestamp=2187.0)

action_items.description: Email Victoria re: AirOps use on vendor pages
Draft renewal proposal for Victoria: annual, no opt-out, flexible scope, 10/wk CFD + 5–10/wk ramp.com; then send/have Mara send
